ety: prob. S. सत्यं; whence H. सई, q.v.
def: part. Yea, verily, indeed, true enough, forsooth; just so; very well, so be it, let it be; just; pray; please; (often added to the particle to, e.g. āʼo to sahī, 'Just come then,' 'come if you dare'; — kholo to sahī, 'Pray open,' 'do open').
more: (perhaps for the Arab. صحيح) An emphatical particle, (it is) true or correct or good; very well!
(improp. sihi ) 1. adj. Straight. 2. s. f. A straight cypress tree. سہي قد sahī-ḳadd, adj. Like the cypress in stature.

def: adv. S. अस to be. Let it be! be it so! 1. Yehī sahī . 2. Murde par sau man maṭṭī to ek man aur bhī sahī . Prov. A hundred maunds of earth heaped on the dead, a maund more heap as well upon his narrow bed. 3. Āo to sahī! Come near if you dare!
n. f. S. शल्लकी A porcupine.
adj. Erect (as a cypress), straight; — s.f. A straight cypress-tree
subs: adj. Like a cypress in stature, erect and graceful as the cypress.

def: adj. Possessing, possessed of, endowed with (used in comp.; see below); — s.m. Companion, associate, comrade; possessor, owner, lord, great man, governor, chief; (in some Hindī dialects) God; — a gentleman, a European gentleman; a title of courtesy, Master, Mr., Sir
more: n. m. sāhibah , n. f. Pl. as'hāb . صحب kept company. 1. A companion; an associate. 2. ( In comp .) Owner; possessor, as sāhib-i-takht , 3. ( sāhebā ) Husband ( patī ). Hukkā mat pīve, sāhebā? maiṅ barjat hūṅ toë, Āṅkhan andho hoëyā, log takeṅge moë . Dohā. Dont huqqa smoke, my spouse, I tell you, dont, I pray, You will be blind, then men will ogle me, I say. 4. Sovereign; ruler; chief; governor. 5. A gentleman; a European gentleman. 6. God ( parmeshvar ). 1. Haivān, pakherū, nar, nārī, kyā būṛhā, bālak, bachchā hai, Kul ālam terī yād kare, tū sāhab sab kā sachchā hai . Hymn. ety: v.n. fr. صحّ 'to be healthy, or sound,' &c.
def: adj. Healthy, sound, valid; perfect; whole, entire; substantial, real, true, genuine; pure; right, just, proper, correct, accurate, authentic, sure, certain; — s.f. Signature, sign or mark, attestation; sanction (syn. taṣdīq)
more: adj. 1. Correct. 2. See تَهيک (1—8, 13—15). 3. Perfect; whole; integral. 4. Sound; healthy. sahīh , n. f. 1. Signature; sign or mark; attestation. 2. Sanction ( tasdīq ). sahīh-ul-aql . Sane; of sound mind. sahīh-ul-mizāj . In perfect health. sahīh-un-nasab , adj. Legitimate. sahīh aur pukhtah . Good and valid. sahīh aur durust aql . Sanity ( ṭhīk samajh ). sahīh dāvā , n. m. A just claim. sahīh sālim, yā salāmat , adj. Safe and sound. sahīh sālim, yā salāmat rahnā , v. n. To keep safe and sound. sahīh sālim k. , v. a. To restore to health; heal. sahīh farz k. , v. a. To assume or accept as true. sahīh qarār denā yā tajvīz k. , v. a. To determine or receive as genuine or valid. sahīh k. , v. a. 1. See ṭhīk k. , and biṭhānā (1, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 34, 35). 2. To sign ( dastkhat k .). 3. To enter in one's account; to book; register. Siyāhe meṅ sahī kar lo . 4. To strike; hit. Chāṅṭā sahī kīy…
(from صحّ) adj. Accurate, just, pure, perfect, entire, sound, certain, authentick, correct, genuine. صحيح کرنا ṣaḥīḥ karnā, 1. To sign. 2. to correct.
subs: adj. & adv. Safe and sound, safe and well
(-ko), To determine, or to receive, as genuine or valid

v.t. To make sound, &c.; to make well, to cure, heal; to rectify, set right, regulate, correct, adjust, mend; to complete, finish, perfect; to render valid, to substantiate, to establish, to verify; to make certain, to determine (syn. ṭhīk karnā; durust karnā); — to vouchsafe, give, or deal (a slap, &c.), to strike, hit (e.g. tamāṅća ṣaḥīḥ karnā); — to write one's signature (in, -meṅ), to sign (a document, &c.); to enter (in an account, &c.), to book, register
adj. Of pure or genuine descent; legitimate
v.n. To be rectified, be corrected; to be confirmed, be substantiated, &c.; — to be signed; be attested, &c.
It is right, or true, &c.; true; all right; quite correct; exactly, precisely (syn. durust).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَحِیح (sahīh), from Arabic صَحِيح (ṣaḥīḥ).

def: v. n. To be afraid. Maiṅ paṛī thī, achānak ūpar chaṛh āyo, Jab utro tab pasīnā āyo, Maiṅ sahm gaī, muṅh se niksī na pukār. 'E, sakhī, sājan?' 'Nā, sakhī, bukhār' . Riddle. Mukrī . I prostrate lay, he all at once upon me came, When he left me a sweat ran over all my frame, With fear I was o'erpowered, I could not scream or shout. 'A lover, dear?' 'No, dear, of fever I'd a bout.' sahmā sahmā, sahmī sahmī , adj. Frightened. Woh to kuchh sahmī sahmī hai .
adj. See s.v. sahm. s.m. An arrow (for shooting or for casting lots); a lot, share, portion; transverse beam (of a house or chamber); segment (of a circle, &c.); versed sine (of an arc).

s. m. 1. A court-yard, an area. 2. A large cup or goblet. 3. A small dish or plate.

ety: sah˚ = Prk. सह(इ) = S. सह(ते), rt. सह्
def: v.t. To bear, support; to suffer; endure, tolerate, put up with; — v.n. To be borne; to be digested, be assimilated, to agree (with, — as food, &c.).
more: v. n. To bear, to endure, to support.

def: v. a. S. सह 1. To support (اتَهانا 19). 1. Bhūk choṭ to sahī na jāve, dhūp tapat sah jāūṅgā, Tājī roṭī po de, mātā! bāsī nāhīṅ khāūṅgā . Rus. The pinch of hunger bear I cant, I can the solar heat, Fresh bread, my mother, bake for me! stale bread I will not eat. 2. Sau gāṛī ṭoṭā sahūṅ, mo pe bālā sahyo na jāë! Din kaṭe mo ko aur rāt chhai māhī jāë! Wom. Dohā. Loss hundred cart-loads I could bear, but not for spouse a child, The day wears on, the night as heavy drags as six months piled. 2. To bear meekly (an affront). Ādmī par jaisī paṛtī hai vaisī sahtā hai . Prov. Whate'er befals one bears. 3. To allow; tolerate. 4. To be digested, assimilated ( pachnā 1); to agree with (food). sah'tā , H; karm , S; mafūl , A. n. m. The objective case. sah'tā , adj. 1. Bearable; endurable. Sahtā sahe, an-sahtā chhātī dahe . Prov. What may be borne it borne will be, what cannot be upon the breast will lie. 2. Lukewarm. Sahtā sahtā dūdh pīyo! sahtā-batāū kām ; A. fel-i-majhūl ; S. कर्मप्रधान क्रिया ( Gram .) A passive verb.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To shine; to beseem, to become.

def: s.f. corr. of shahnāʼī, q.v. s.f. Superintendence; the police.

ety: corr. of P. صحنك, dim. of A. صحن

def: s. A small earthen pot.
s.f. A small dish or plate; a small earthen pot, &c. (vulg. sahnak); — an offering or oblation to Fātima.
more: 1. A small earthen dish or plate. Abahīṅ to bhar sanhak māṛ pīle heā, kā bhāt bhāt kaile heṅ? Mag. Just now you drank a dish full of congy, and you are crying out for rice! 2. (Mah. Wom.) An offering to Fātima, of which chaste women only may partake. It is often placed at a crossroad to appease an evil spirit. Hūṅ maile sir se, sir mujhe dhonā zurūr hai, Sahnak meṅ shāmil, aë buā! honā zurūr hai . Wom. Rāhat. I've had the monthly flow, I needs must wash my head, I must eat of the dish for women chaste out spread.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Co-uterine, born of one mother; of the whole blood (kindred); — a brother of the whole blood, uterine brother
more: adj. Born of one mother; as, سہودربهاٴي sahodar-bhāʼī, Full brother.

Khiṛkī khol muhal meṅ paiṭhī, chuṭki sain bujhātī thī . The wicket opened she into the palace went, A fillip with her fingers for a message sent. sain mārnā, denā, yā chalānā , v. a. 1. To wink; make a sign with the eye. Makṛī merī māosī jin jālā pūrā, Karkaiṅṭā merā bairī jin sain chalāī. Hāë Hasan! Hāë Hasan! Rus Mah. Wom. A dirge. A web for him the spider wove—dear to me as my aunt, The lizard is my foe who did betray him recreant. Alas Hasan! Alas Hasan! 2. To wanton with one's eyes; to ogle. Man rājā, aur mantrī nainā, Mil-kar dou chalāvat sainā . Rāmāyan. The heart's a king, his ministers the eyes, By signs you of their wishes they apprise. sainoṅ meṅ, sainoṅ se , adv. Poverty goes a marketing! 4. Jest ( tamāsha 1). Kyā sair huī hai! 5. Perusal; reading. Kitāb kī sair . 6. A spectacle; view; scene; landscape. sair k. , v. a. 1. To take the air. 2. To make an excursion, etc. 3. To read; peruse. 4. To enjoy sights, etc. sair-gāh , n. f. 1. A place for recreation. 2. A place affording a good view. sair o shikār . Shooting and hunting.

A descendant of Ḥusain, who was the grandson of the prophet Muḥammad. سيد الانام saiyidu-l-anām (the prince of men and of all living creatures), سيد البشر saiyidu-l-bashar (the prince of mortals), سيد المرسلين يا انبيا saiyidu-l-mursalīn or -ambiyā (the head of the apostles, or prophets) are epithets of Muḥammad.

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡 (sajjaṇa), from Sanskrit सज्जन (sajjana, “good man”), from सत् (sát) + जन (jána).

def: s.m. Preparing, dressing, decorating, adorning.
more: n. m. S. सज्जन from सत् true, जन man 1. Husband; lord; master. 1. Mā, āë sājan le chale, Merā terā bas nahīṅ chaltā rī mā! Hin. Girls' song. O mother! now my spouse is come, he taketh me away, No power in me nor you our separation sad can stay! 2. Sājan! woh din kaunsā, jo sukh se lāë pīt, Ab dukh de niyāre bhaë, kaun gāoṅ kī rīt? My spouse! the day hath been when love to me brought gladness here, And now to go away and give me pain doth strange appear! 3. Kar le siṅgār chatar albelī! sājan ke ghar jānā hogā, Vahīṅ terā pīhar, vahīṅ terā sahurā, vahīṅ terā pīv nimānā hogā . Masty. Adorn yourself, gay sprightly nymph! to your lord's house you must go! Your father-in-law, and house, and tender spouse there you shall know. 2. A sweetheart; lover.
s. m. A sweetheart, a lover.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡 (sajjaṇa), from Sanskrit सज्जन (sajjana, “good man”), from सत् (sát) + जन (jána).

def: v.t. To cause (a person) to prepare (a thing); to arrange, prepare, assort, methodize; to decorate, adorn; to mend
more: v. a. S. सज्ज 1. To assort; sort; settle ( tartīb k .). 2. To adorn ( ārāstah k .). Ūdālaṅhgā, jarad oḍhnā, paṅch raṅg āṅguṭlā dūṅgā! Tane upar-sī ḍhāl pharā-kar latte, ṭūm sajā dūṅgā . Rus. Wom. song. Mauve skirt, wrap yellow, boddice of five colors I'll bring you, A veil o'er you bedecked I'll throw, a fairy bright to view.
(caus. of سجنا sajnā ) v. a. To cause to prepare.
subs: v.t. To put into a state of preparation; to arrange, order, to set out; to adorn.

Hans denā har dam nāz-bharā, dikhlānā saj dhaj shokhī kā, Har gālī misrī qand bharī, har ek qadam aṭkhelī kā, Dil shād kīyā, aur moh līyā, yeh joban pāyā Holī ne! Nazīr. With laughter constant, blandishments and piquant charms displayed, Abuse too showered as candy sweet, each step the wanton swayed The heart made glad, the senses tranced, such beauty Holī made!

ety: sajā(nā) + Prk. एअव्वं and एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्यं, and (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं

ety: Prk. सज्झओ; S. साह्य, or सह्य (rt. सह्)+कः
def: s.m. Association, company (in trade), partnership; relation, connection; — share (in a company); — adj. Joint
more: n. m. S. साहाय्य. 1. An association; a company; joint concern; community of sharers; partnership. 2. A share. 1. Sājhe kī Holī sab se bhalī . Prov. The carnival of Holi best in company. 2. Sakhī kī kamāī meṅ sab kā sājhā . Prov. In the generous man's earnings all have a share. 3. Sājhe kī mā Gaṅgā na pāve . Prov. The mother who hath joint-heirs left, of Ganges' healing virtues is bereft. (Each one leaves to. the others the expense of conveying her body to the Ganges and feasting the Brahmans). 4. Sājhā jorū aur khasam hīkā achchhā hai . Prov. The best partnership is that between man and wife. 5. Tel dāl kamlī kā sājhā! Prov. He brought a little oil, in the jack fruit he a share must have! 6. Sājhe kī haṅḍyā chaurāhe meṅ phūṭe . Prov. The partners' cooking pot is broken on the public cross-road. (When partners break their affairs become public). 7. Sājhā sadhe na bāp kā, saë rāse kī khān;…
subs: (-kā), Partnership to be dissolved; connection to be severed; — sājhā ćhūṭnā, Dissolution of partnership

To enter into partnership; to form a company. sājhe, sājhe meṅ , adv. Jointly; in partnership. nafe o nuqsān kā sājhī , A partner in gain and loss.
subs: v.n. To be or become a partner, &c. ( = sājhā karnā, q.v.).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Well-born, of good family, well-bred, respectable, reputable; virtuous, good; — a virtuous or good man; a respectable man, one of good family; — a sweetheart, lover; husband.
more: n. m. S. सज्जन from सत true जन man. 1. A respectable person; a good or virtuous man; a gentleman. Rām rāj-abhishek suni hīye harkhe nar nārī, Lagī sumaṅgal sajan sab Bidhī anukul bichār . Rāmāyan. 2. A sweetheart; lover; husband. Sajan, tum jhūṭ mat bolo! Khudā ko sāṅch piyārā hai, Kahāvat hai baḍoṅ kī yūn, 'kadhī sāṅchā na hārā hai .' My husband, do not tell a lie! God loves the truth, you know, It hath been said of old 'the truthful ne'er hath come to woe.'
s. m. f. A respectable person, a sweetheart, a mistress.

def: s.m. Fastening on (clothes), dressing; putting on armour, arming, preparing; — a guard, sentry, picquet; a ferry (syn. ghāṭ).
s.f. = sajanī, q.v.

s.m. = sajjan, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Well-born, of good family, well-bred, respectable, reputable; virtuous, good; — a virtuous or good man; a respectable man, one of good family; — a sweetheart, lover; husband.
s.f. The act of dressing; decoration; ornamenting or caparisoning an elephant.
s.f. A virtuous woman; a respectable woman; a mistress, sweetheart; a female friend (syn. sahelī).
more: n. f. A female friend. 1. Uṭh rī! Holī ho rahī! tū kyā paṛī sove rī! Rain gaī to jāne de, sajnī, din mat khove rī! Song. Holī . Get up! 'tis Holī! this is not a time for sleep, So be 't the night is past, the day in revels keep! 2. Sun sun, sajnī, kaise kaṭegī rajnī! Pī par jāūṅ, khaṛī pachhtāūṅ, pāël merī bajnī . Song. Lāunī . Hear, hear, my dear! how ever shall I pass the night? If to my spouse I go I shall repent it quite, My anklets clink with every motion e'er so slight.

def: s.f. Natron, impure carbonate of soda, a kind of mineral alkali (used in India instead of soap for cleaning linen, &c.; syn. khār)
more: n. f. S. सर्ज्जो The Alkaline produce of a plant, rāj bhaṅg or rehe , q. v. ( Coroxylon Griffithū ) obtained from its ashes when burnt; fuller's earth. Three qualities are distinguished— choā-sajjī , the purest; hathā-sajjī , the second quality; and kara-sajjī , the most impure. All these are varieties of the barilla or soda of commerce.
s. f. A kind of mineral alkali, natron, impure carbonate of soda.

def: adj. & s.m. = saṅjhlā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), The third (of four brothers, &c.); of the third rank or degree; — the third of four brothers.
more: s. m. Third brother of four or more.

ety: sāj˚ = Prk. सज्ज(इ) = S. सज्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. संज्
def: v.t. To prepare, to fit out; to dress, decorate, bedeck; to regulate; — v.n. ( = sajnā), To be prepared, &c.; to be accoutred; — to become, fit, suit.
more: 1. v. a. To prepare, dress, decorate, bedeck, regulate. 2. v. n. To become, fit, suit.

def: v.t. To arrange, adjust, rectify; to prepare, dress, accoutre; to ornament; to ornament, adorn.
more: v. n. 1. See sajānā , v. a. 2. To fit; become; beseem. 3. To be accoutred.
1. v. n. To be prepared. 2. To fit, to become, to beseem. 3. v. a. To dress, to ornament, to accoutre, to adjust, to rectify. 4. s. m. See سجن sajan.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), = sa-jnānī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Possessed of knowledge, knowing, intelligent, wise, sagacious; — an intelligent man, a wise man, &c.
s.f. Intelligence, sagacity, wisdom.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Possessed of knowledge, knowing, intelligent, wise, sagacious; — an intelligent man, a wise man, &c.
s.f. corr. of sva-jnāti, q.v. s.f. One's own kindred or kin; — s.m.f. Kinsman, relation.
part. (f. -ā), Known, apprehended, perceived.
s.m.f. Possessed of youth, in the prime of youth, in the enjoyment of youth, youthful; — one who is experiencing the season or the condition of youth.
v.t. To cause to be prepared, to have or get prepared, to have arranged; to have rectified, &c.
more: (caus. of سجنا sajnā ) v. a. To cause to prepare.

def: s.f. = śayyā or sej, qq.v.; — 2˚ s.m. = sahajnā, q.v.; — 3˚ s.m. corr. of sajīya, q.v.
s.f. corr. of śayyā, q.v.
s.f. The leg (from the ankle to the knee), the shank; — trunk, shaft, bole (of a tree); stalk (of a herb)
more: (Heb. from ساق Heb. To drive, to impel, to run) s. f. 1. The leg. 2. The trunk of a tree, the stalk of an herb. ساق عروس sāḳ-i-aﻌrūs, s. A kind of sweetmeat.
subs: s.f. lit. 'Bride's leg'; a sort of sweet bread.

def: s.m. Pot-herbs, greens ( = sāg, q.v.)
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. = شك śaka or śak, q.v. s.m. Era, epoch (generally applied to the era of Sālivāhan, reckoning from about 78 ¼ of the Christian era)
subs: v.n. To establish an era; (met.) to distinguish oneself by heroic actions
s.m. A king who has established an era ( = śākeśvar).

def: s.f. Ability, capacity, strength, power, authority.
s.f. corr. of شك shakk, q.v. adj. Difficult, hard, distressing, grievous, burdensome, afflicting, troublesome, severe, perplexing, embarrassing, wearisome, &c.
s.m. Era, epoch (generally applied to the era of Sālivāhan, reckoning from about 78 ¼ of the Christian era)
more: s. m. An era. ساکاکرنا sākā karnā, To establish an era; ( met. ) To distinguish oneʼs self by heroick actions. ساکي بندهہ sāke-bandh, s. m. A king who has established an era.
subs: v.n. To establish an era; (met.) to distinguish oneself by heroic actions
s.m. A king who has established an era ( = śākeśvar).

def: s.m. Might, power, main.
wety: Either from Classical Persian سکا (sakā) or a modern learned borrowing from an ancient Indo-Iranian language referring to the same nomadic peoples.

def: s.m. Any edible leaf, or fruit, or root, or herb; a vegetable, pot-herb, greens ( = sāg, q.v.); — the teak tree, Tectona grandis; — power, strength, energy, force; — name of the sixth of the seven dvīpas or divisions of the world (surrounded by the white or milky sea); — an era, a period (usually dating from the reign of some celebrated prince; and most generally applied to the era of Śālivāhan)
subs: (˚ka+īś˚), s.m. An epithet of any prince (as Yudhishṭhir, Vikramāditya, or Śālivāhan) who has established an era, or after whom an era is named.

def: s.m. A sovereign or prince who gives his name to an era (esp. applied to Śālivāhan); — an era, epoch (esp. that of the monarch Śālivāhan, commencing 76 or 78 years after the Christian era); — name of a particular tribe or race of people (regarded by some as the followers or the descendants of Śālivāhan)
subs: (˚ka+ab˚), s.m. A year of the śaka era.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَک (šak), borrowed from Arabic شَكّ (šakk).

def: adj. All, entire, the whole, every, each and all
more: adj. S. सकल from स with, कला part. Each and all ( sārā ). Sakal mahā-jan kā partāp badho, mahrāj! (Said by a barber after uṭhāonī , q. v.)

adj. 1. White. سکل پچهہ sukal pachchh̤ ( शुक्लपक्ष ) s. m. The light half of a month, the fifteen days from new to full moon. 2. A title of Brāhmaṇs. ¶ sakkal, s. f. (dakh.) A chain: see سانکر sānkar.
subs: s.m. lit. 'Dwelling in all hearts'; an epithet of Vishnu.

def: s.m. Name of a school of the Rig-veda (supposed to have been founded by the elder Śākalya); — s.f. A mixture of sesasum seeds, barley, ghī, coarse sugar, fruits, &c., used by Hindūs in oblations to the gods.
more: s. f. A mixture of sesamum seed, barley, ghī, coarse sugar, fruits, &c. used in oblations to the gods.

def: s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
s.f. = śākal, q.v. s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
s.m. pl. Name of a tribe inhabiting Bhārat-varsha; the followers of the Śākala school of the Veda.
adj. (dakh.) All: see سکل sakal.

ety: for A. ثقالة, inf. n. of ثقل 'to be heavy'
def: s.f. Heaviness, weight; heavy load, indigestion ( = s̤iql).
more: n. f. ثقل was heavy. Heaviness; weight. Bachche ke peṭ meṅ siqālat hai . Mah.
wety: From Classical Persian ثقالت (saqālat), from Arabic verbal noun ثَقَالَة (ṯaqāla), from verb root ثَقُلَ (ṯaqula, “to be heavy”).

def: adv. Betimes, early in the morning, at dawn.
s.f. Early morning, dawn ( = sakāl); — adv. Early in the morning ( = sakāle).
v.t. = sakārnā, q.v. v.t. To accept, or endorse as accepted (a bill)
subs: s.m. Accepter, or endorser (of a bill)

def: s.m. Totality, entireness, completeness; the whole, all.
corr. of sākalya, q.v. s.m. Totality, entireness, completeness; the whole, all.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Contemed satisfied, gratified, happy; full of love, loving; in love; sensual, lascivious; — done with a desire of future reward or fruition; — one who has attained his desire, a contented person; a lover, &c.
adj. Engaged, occupied, busy.
s.f. Name of a city (supposed to be the modern Sāmbhar in Ajmer).
s.m. A kind of fossil salt brought from a lake in the vicinity of the town Sāmbhar.
fem. of sāqī, q.v.
more: n. f. A female who prepares tobacco, bhaṅg , etc. for customers; a barmaid.

def: s.m. A dwelling, residence (syn. maskan).

ety: prob. fr. S. शुक्, rt. सुच्
def: v.n. To be sorrowful; to grieve, mourn, repine; to be wearied or languid.
more: v. n. To be sorrowful, to be wearied.

def: (for A. سکنة sakanat, pl. of sākin), s.m. pl. Inhabitants, residents.
s.f. pl. (of saknat), Rests, pauses, &c.
